---
title: "gibbs_ringing"
generated: true
description: "Inject Gibbs (truncation) ringing by truncating high-frequency k-space."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.augment.intensity`](../nitrix-augment-intensity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gibbs_ringing -->
```python
gibbs_ringing(x: Float[Array, '...'], alpha: float, *, axes: Optional[Sequence[int]] = None) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gibbs_ringing -->
Inject Gibbs (truncation) ringing by truncating high-frequency k-space.

Gibbs ringing is the oscillation that appears near sharp edges when a
signal's spectrum is sharply truncated -- the artefact of a
finitely-sampled (band-limited) acquisition.  This models it directly:
transform to k-space, zero every frequency whose normalised radius
exceeds `1 - alpha` (a hard spherical truncation -- the sharp cutoff
*is* what produces the ringing), and transform back.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gibbs_ringing -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` | — | Real-valued input. |
| `alpha` | `float` | — | — | Truncation strength in `[0, 1]`. `0` keeps the full spectrum (identity); larger values remove more high-frequency content and ring more strongly; `1` keeps only the DC component. Deterministic in `alpha` -- draw it from a range in the caller for a random augmentation. |
| `axes` | `Optional[Sequence[int]]` | — | `None` | Axes to transform over. `None` (default) uses all axes; for a channels-last volume pass the spatial axes so the channel axis is not mixed into the transform. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gibbs_ringing -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
